invariants: The floating grid is not promoted to a certified theorem.
The intervalization target is finite-grid only until a continuum-in-T bridge is proved.
The proposed error cap is sufficient target bookkeeping, not a proved bound.
Endpoint PF, RH, Laguerre-Polya membership, and Lambda <= 0 are forbidden as inputs.

matrix_rows.claim: Import the cancellation-reduced finite-grid ratios and compute remaining ratio slack to the first-omitted threshold one.

certification_budget_rows.proof_boundary: Certification budget only; the intervalized quadrature and analytic error terms are not yet proved.
Certification budget only; the intervalized quadrature and analytic error terms are not yet proved.

obligation_rows.claim: Use the cancellation-reduced Gamma-expectation residual cores for value and derivative channels.
obligation_rows.proof_boundary: Exact coordinate identity only; not a numerical enclosure.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: None for the identity; downstream proof must preserve the same normalization and degree-40 polynomial.

obligation_rows.claim: Replace SciPy floating generalized Laguerre nodes and weights by interval enclosures for every recorded index and order.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: Certified roots and Christoffel weights for L_N^(i-1/2), including root separation and weight-radius propagation.

obligation_rows.claim: Enclose Phi, Phi', Phi(0), and their n>30 tails on all node-induced v ranges used by the finite grid.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: Arb or analytic exponential-tail enclosure for value and derivative cores after the n=30 truncation.

obligation_rows.claim: Bound the generalized Gauss-Laguerre quadrature remainder for the cancellation-reduced cores, not just the spread across orders.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: A rigorous quadrature remainder theorem or an interval adaptive integral cross-check for the Gamma expectation.

obligation_rows.claim: Propagate Arb coefficient-ratio balls and first-omitted-term denominators through the ratio comparisons.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: Interval-safe denominator lower bounds and signed residual absolute-value enclosure in first-omitted units.

obligation_rows.claim: Account for arithmetic rounding, summation, and cross-source aggregation so the total ratio error remains below the proposed cap.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: A machine-checkable error ledger whose total ratio radius is below the recorded total cap.

obligation_rows.claim: An intervalized finite T grid would by itself prove the full collar residual theorem on 0<=u<=1/1156.
obligation_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected promotion only; finite grid certification is not a uniform collar theorem.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: A separate T-continuity, monotonicity, interval subdivision, or far-tail theorem is still required.

obligation_rows.claim: A promoted proof must state all interval sources, cap their total ratio error, and separately bridge from finite grid certification to the full collar or all-k theorem.
obligation_rows.proof_boundary: Proof-hygiene gate only; not scaled-curvature monotonicity, cone entry, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
obligation_rows.required_upgrade: Intervalized grid certificate plus a continuum-in-T theorem or subdivision plan with certified coverage.

diagnostics.proof_boundary_note: This target is a certification roadmap for the finite cancellation-reduced grid. It does not provide node/weight intervals, quadrature-remainder bounds, Phi n-tail bounds, or a continuum-in-T theorem.

matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Exact budget bookkeeping only; not an interval certificate.

matrix_rows.claim: If all intervalization and quadrature-certification errors are bounded by the proposed total ratio cap in both channels, the finite grid remains below one first omitted term.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Sufficient finite-grid certification condition only; the cap is not yet proved.

matrix_rows.claim: The required interval sources are Laguerre node/weight balls, Phi and Phi' n-tail balls, coefficient-ratio propagation, quadrature-remainder error, and aggregation/rounding control.
A certified finite T grid still requires a separate continuum-in-T or far-tail theorem before it can become the full real-collar residual estimate.
The existing floating cancellation-reduced grid already proves the first-omitted-term theorem.
matrix_rows.gap: The grid lacks interval node/weight enclosures, quadrature-remainder bounds, Phi n-tail bounds, and T-continuum coverage.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected promotion only; not a proof of the actual remainder.

matrix_rows.claim: Any promoted remainder proof must pass the interval-source ledger and keep total ratio radii below the recorded cap before using the grid as certified evidence.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Proof-hygiene gate only; not scaled-curvature monotonicity, cone entry, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

proof_boundary: Open numerical-certification target only. It gives a sufficient error budget for certifying the finite cancellation-reduced grid, but it does not provide interval enclosures, does not prove a uniform collar remainder theorem, does not prove scaled-curvature monotonicity, does not prove cone entry, and does not prove RH or Lambda <= 0.

summary.main_finding: The cancellation-reduced finite grid leaves a common ratio slack of about 2.928994097967e-2 before reaching one first omitted term. A future interval certificate with total ratio error below 1.0e-2 in both value and derivative channels would keep the finite grid below the first-omitted threshold. The remaining work is to intervalize Laguerre nodes/weights, Phi tails, coefficient propagation, quadrature error, and then separately bridge the finite grid to the full collar.
